key_highlights: Stocks hit record highs as oil falls on US-Iran hopes, but gas prices surge to $4.39/gallon in biggest one-day jump since Iran ceasefire.
Spirit Airlines shuts operations after White House bailout collapses, impacting thousands of employees and travelers.
Gold notches second weekly loss as US-Iran talks hang in limbo, but Deutsche Bank predicts $8,000 by 2031.
Yen soars after Japan intervenes following final warning; emerging assets climb on Iran's new proposal.
Big Tech earnings test record rally as AI spending takes center stage; Apple climbs on earnings, Sandisk skids.
Pentagon strikes deals with seven Big Tech companies for AI; AI investment boosted economic growth.
Rivian shares decline despite Q1 beat and expanded Georgia plant plans; Roblox tumbles 22% premarket after cutting bookings forecast.
Columbia risks losing top credit rating as Trump slams colleges; private credit faces fresh concerns.
Air Canada pulls 2026 outlook on fuel costs; shares drop.
Uber adds hotel bookings and vacation rentals to become a one-stop shop for travel.

sectors.trend: AI spending continues to dominate, with Big Tech earnings testing record rally. Apple climbs on earnings, Sandisk skids despite strong Q3. Meta partners with CoreWeave for AI cloud. Palantir expands AI at USDA. Pentagon signs AI deals with seven Big Tech companies. AI investment boosted economic growth.
sectors.implications: AI infrastructure and cloud growth remain strong tailwinds for tech, but valuation concerns persist. Companies with clear AI monetization (Meta, Palantir, CoreWeave) are favored. Roblox's cut in bookings forecast signals consumer spending caution.

sectors.trend: Oil prices fall on US-Iran hopes, but gas prices surge to $4.39/gallon. Big Oil bosses warn energy is moving closer to cliff's edge. Air Canada pulls 2026 outlook on fuel costs. Canaccord upgrades Gran Tierra Energy to Buy.
sectors.implications: Geopolitical tensions (Iran) and supply constraints keep energy volatile. Short-term relief from Iran talks may be fleeting. Energy stocks (GTE) offer value, but caution on fuel-sensitive sectors (airlines).

sectors.trend: Spirit Airlines shuts operations after bailout collapse, impacting thousands. Air Canada pulls outlook on fuel costs. Uber adds hotel bookings and vacation rentals to become one-stop travel shop.
sectors.implications: Spirit's shutdown signals distress in low-cost carriers. Fuel costs and consumer spending caution weigh on airlines. Uber's expansion into travel could disrupt traditional OTAs.

sectors.trend: Multiple analyst initiations and upgrades: Cantor Fitzgerald initiates Shattuck Labs, UBS bullish on Amneal Pharmaceuticals, H.C. Wainwright bullish on Century Therapeutics and Adlai Nortye. Abbott receives FDA clearance for next-gen software. Medtronic gets CE Mark for surgical system. Neurocrine presents new data. Truist cuts PT on iRhythm and Stryker.
sectors.implications: Biotech continues to see selective bullish coverage, especially in oncology and rare diseases. MedTech shows steady innovation. However, PT cuts on some names suggest caution on valuation.

sectors.trend: West Marine readies possible bankruptcy to close stores. Build-A-Bear recalls 36,000 bears. Clorox Q3 earnings call highlights. Brooks Apparel resonates with Chinese runners. Gas prices hit $4.39/gallon, impacting consumer spending.
sectors.implications: Consumer discretionary faces headwinds from inflation and gas prices. Retail bankruptcies (West Marine) and recalls (Build-A-Bear) indicate stress. Brooks' success in China shows niche opportunities.

sectors.trend: Rivian shares decline despite Q1 beat and expanded Georgia plant plans. GM bets on trucks and keeps writing down EVs amid trade unease. Senators introduce bipartisan bill to ban Chinese vehicles and auto parts.
sectors.implications: EV sector faces headwinds from trade tensions and GM's EV write-downs. Rivian's beat not enough to offset macro concerns. Ban on Chinese parts could reshape supply chains.

sectors.trend: Fresh concerns for private credit as Milken looms. Pershing Square IPO investors post gain after free shares. Stocks hit record highs. Fast-money hedge fund trades roar.
sectors.implications: Private credit market faces scrutiny amid rising rates and potential defaults. IPO market shows signs of life (Pershing Square, Cerebras). Hedge fund activity suggests short-term trading opportunities.

sectors.trend: Gold notches second weekly loss as US-Iran talks hang in limbo. Deutsche Bank predicts gold to hit $8,000 by 2031. Blue Moon Metals consolidates tungsten claims.
sectors.implications: Gold's short-term weakness due to Iran talks, but long-term bullish outlook remains. Tungsten and other critical minerals gain attention.

sectors.trend: LA's Television City studio lot faces sale forced by lenders. Columbia risks losing top credit rating. Aeva expands AI-powered smart infrastructure deployment.
sectors.implications: Commercial real estate stress continues. Infrastructure spending and AI integration offer growth areas.

key_themes: Geopolitical tensions (US-Iran) driving energy and gold volatility
AI investment boom driving tech sector growth and Pentagon contracts
Consumer spending caution amid high gas prices and inflation
Airlines in distress (Spirit shutdown, Air Canada outlook) due to fuel costs
Biotech selective bullish coverage with pipeline catalysts
Record stock market rally driven by Big Tech earnings and AI optimism
Japan intervention in FX markets (yen soars)
Trade tensions with China (auto parts ban, tariffs)
Retail bankruptcies and recalls signaling consumer stress

risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify into energy and gold; hedge with options on oil futures; reduce exposure to airlines and fuel-sensitive sectors.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Consumer spending slowdown due to high gas prices and inflation
risk_assessment.mitigation: Focus on defensive consumer staples (Clorox, Abbott) and discount retailers; avoid discretionary names like West Marine, Spirit Airlines.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Airlines industry distress (Spirit shutdown, Air Canada outlook)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid airline stocks; consider short positions on carriers with high fuel exposure; monitor for contagion to other low-cost carriers.
Reduce exposure to private credit funds; favor high-quality corporate bonds; monitor BDC and CLO performance.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Trade tensions with China (auto parts ban, tariffs)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify supply chains; favor companies with domestic production; avoid Chinese auto parts suppliers.
Hedge FX exposure for international portfolios; consider yen-denominated assets if intervention stabilizes; monitor emerging market flows.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Tech valuation bubble (AI spending, record rally)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Take profits on high-flying AI names; rotate into value and dividend stocks; use trailing stops on tech positions.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory and political risks (Columbia credit rating, FCC, prediction markets)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Monitor regulatory changes in education, media, and financial markets; avoid overexposure to sectors under political scrutiny.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy AI infrastructure plays (CoreWeave, Palantir, Meta) on pullbacks
opportunities.rationale: AI spending continues to accelerate with Pentagon deals and hyperscaler leases. CoreWeave's partnership with Meta and Palantir's USDA expansion indicate strong demand.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate gold on dips for long-term hedge
opportunities.rationale: Deutsche Bank predicts $8,000/oz by 2031. Geopolitical tensions and inflation support gold. Short-term weakness from Iran talks is a buying opportunity.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy selected biotech names with strong catalysts (Shattuck Labs, Amneal, Century Therapeutics)
opportunities.rationale: Analyst initiations and upgrades indicate pipeline value. Shattuck Labs (STTK) and Amneal (AMRX) have bullish coverage. Century Therapeutics (IPSC) has best-in-class potential.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in energy stocks (Gran Tierra Energy) on upgrades and supply constraints
opportunities.rationale: Canaccord upgrades GTE to Buy with raised PT. Energy sector benefits from supply constraints and geopolitical risk premium.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Uber on travel expansion strategy
opportunities.rationale: Uber adding hotel bookings and vacation rentals positions it as a one-stop travel shop, potentially disrupting OTAs. Strong growth catalyst.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to airlines and fuel-sensitive sectors
defensive_moves.rationale: Spirit shutdown and Air Canada outlook cut signal industry distress. High fuel costs and consumer caution will pressure earnings.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against consumer spending slowdown with staples and healthcare
defensive_moves.rationale: High gas prices and inflation are squeezing consumers. Clorox, Abbott, and Medtronic offer defensive growth.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to private credit and high-yield bonds
defensive_moves.rationale: Fresh concerns about private credit market stress. Archroma's delayed loan extension signals tightening credit conditions.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Take profits on high-flying AI tech names
defensive_moves.rationale: Record rally and high valuations (Roblox down 22%, Shopify analyst says too soon to buy) suggest caution. Rotate into value.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash allocation or short-term Treasuries
defensive_moves.rationale: Geopolitical uncertainty, inflation, and potential Fed rate cut (Warsh) create volatility. Cash provides optionality.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Markets likely to remain volatile due to US-Iran negotiations, oil price swings, and consumer spending data. Record stock rally may pause as Big Tech earnings are digested. Gold may consolidate before resuming uptrend. Yen intervention could stabilize FX but further volatility expected. Spirit shutdown may weigh on travel sector sentiment.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: AI investment boom and Pentagon contracts will drive tech growth. Gold on track for $8,000 by 2031 per Deutsche Bank. Energy sector remains attractive due to supply constraints. Consumer discretionary faces headwinds from inflation. Private credit market may face defaults. Trade tensions with China could reshape supply chains. Fed rate cuts (Warsh) could boost equities but hurt retirees.
key_catalysts: US-Iran nuclear deal outcome and oil price direction
Big Tech earnings (Apple, Meta, Nvidia) and AI spending commentary
Fed policy signals (Kevin Warsh's expected rate cut)
Spirit Airlines bankruptcy proceedings and contagion risk
Pentagon AI contracts and defense spending
China trade policy and auto parts ban
Gold price movement and inflation data
Private credit market stress and default rates
watch_list: WTI crude oil price and gas prices at pump
Consumer confidence and retail sales data
